name: Recover failed Stripe payments with AI emails (by FlyCode)

parameters.text:
=You are writing a short, friendly, and urgent FAILED PAYMENT email. VARIABLES (may be missing): - firstName: {{ $json.firstName }} - lastName: {{ $json.lastName }} - account_name: {{ $json.account_name }} - customer_email: {{ $json.customer_email }} - hosted_invoice_url: {{ $json.hosted_invoice_url }} - invoice_number: {{ $json.invoice_number }} - invoice_amount: {{ $json.invoice_amount }} - currency: {{ $json.currency }} - description: {{ $json.description }} - type: {{ $json.type }} - billing_reason: {{ $json.billing_reason }} - collection: {{ $json.collection }} GOAL: Politely but clearly convey urgency to pay the failed invoice to avoid interruption/cancellation. SUBSCRIPTION NAME: - If "description" exists, extract the plan name by removing any leading " × " and taking the text before the first "("; trim. Example: "1 × daily_test_2 (at $2.00 / day)" -> "daily_test_2". - Otherwise use "{{ $json.account_name }} subscription". AMOUNT FORMATTING: - Two decimals. - Currency symbol map: usd->$, eur->€, gbp->£; otherwise render as " " (e.g., 12.00 AUD). EMAIL BODY REQUIREMENTS (HTML): - Produce valid, minimal HTML **snippet** (no or tags). Use ASCII only (no smart quotes). - Structure: - Use

for paragraphs; use
only for small line breaks inside a paragraph (e.g., in the signature). - Include: greeting, the subscription/plan name, the invoice number, the formatted amount due, and the consequence of non-payment (interruption/cancellation). - Include ONE clear call-to-action as a **hyperlink button** to {{ $json.hosted_invoice_url }}: Pay invoice Do not paste the raw URL in the body; use only the hyperlink above. - Close with a polite reassurance and a **signature block** like:

Thank you,
{{ $json.account_name }} Billing Team

If account_name is missing, render "Billing Team" only. - Tone: polite, reassuring, action-oriented, concise (about 120–180 words). FALLBACKS: - If any variable is missing, use an empty string. - If the link is missing, set href="#" but still render the button. OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT): - Output ONE valid JSON object ONLY (no code fences, no markdown, no extra keys, no arrays). - Start with "{" and end with "}". - EXACT keys with string values: { "to_email": "...", "email_subject": "...", "email_body": "..." // HTML string; do not escape tags; normal JSON string quoting is OK } - "to_email" MUST be {{ $json.customer_email }} if present, else "". - Subject pattern: "Action required: Payment failed for (Invoice )". - Greeting rule: "Hi {{ $json.firstName }}," else "Hi there,".

options.systemMessage: You are the company’s Billing & Payments Manager. Write a warm, personal one-to-one email to the customer about their field subscription invoice.

parameters.content:
## Code in JavaScript1
extracts fields from `body.data.object` and maps:
- firstName
- lastName (split on first space)
- currency
- customer_email,
- hosted_invoice_url
- invoice_number
- invoice_amount (divide total by 100)
- type
- billing_reason
- collection
- description (falls back to first line item)
- account_name

## Filter non-stripe webhooks
If you might receive non-Stripe posts on the Webhook, add a quick guard in the first Code node to bail if.

## Filter non recurring  subscription invoices  
If — guards the flow so you only email on the exact scenario. And ensures one-off invoices or manual-collection accounts don’t get this email.

## Webhook
Receives Stripe events.

*Path is currently a mock UUID; Don't forget to replace it with the path generated by n8n.*

## Code in JavaScript2
Parses the model’s JSON string returned by AI Agent to a real JSON object (`to_email`, `email_subject`, `email_body`).

## AI Agent + OpenAI Chat Model
Generates the email JSON from Stripe invoice data using the prompt. Returns a single JSON object with keys: `to_email`, `email_subject`, `email_body`.

*Don't forget replace the sender (From) and attach your own Postmark credentials at import time.*

## HTTP Request to POSTMARK
Sends the email using, `From`, `To`, `Subject`, `HtmlBody`, and `MessageStream`. 

*Don't forget replace the sender (From) and attach your own Postmark credentials at import time.*
